metadata.title: Jairo González — Frontend Developer
metadata.description: Frontend Developer specialized in React, Next.js, and TypeScript. I build modern, accessible, high-performance interfaces.

hero.name: Jairo González
hero.taglineStart: I build accessible, fast interfaces with animations that tell a story. Specialized in

projectsSection.title: Things I've built
projectsSection.description: A selection of projects I've worked on over the last few years, from multi-tenant SaaS platforms to richly animated websites. Each one shows a different kind of work.

about.title: How I got here
paragraphs: I started by studying Pharmacy at the University of Havana, where I earned my degree. During those years I also completed the first year of Computer Science, which I left for personal reasons — but my curiosity for programming stayed with me.

I picked it back up through self-directed learning and, over the last 2 years, I've built real web applications, both in collaboration with senior developers and as projects I carried from start to finish: products in production for real businesses, multi-tenant SaaS platforms, and websites with advanced animations.
Today I work from Madrid, specializing in frontend with React and Next.js, and I am available for remote or on-site projects. I care about interfaces that feel fast, accessible, and well designed.

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.description: Have a project in mind, an opening, or just want to say hello? Write to me and I'll reply in under 24 hours.

items.title: Yacht Miami Experience
items.subtitle: Yacht charter platform in production for a real Miami business, with bookings and payments
items.role: Frontend Developer · in collaboration with a senior dev who led the backend
items.description: A yacht charter platform in production for a real business in Miami: a multi-language catalog (EN/ES), an admin panel, and a full booking flow with Stripe payments. I built the frontend and the animations, working in collaboration with a senior developer who led the backend.
highlights: Multi-language catalog (EN/ES) with SEO optimized for local discovery
Booking flow integrated with Stripe payments
Admin panel to manage the catalog and reservations
Auth and media storage with Supabase; smooth animations with Framer Motion

caseStudy.challenge: Give a real Miami yacht charter business a production web presence that felt premium and trustworthy while handling a real booking and payment flow, in two languages, without sacrificing mobile speed.
caseStudy.solution: I built the frontend end to end — the multi-language catalog, the booking flow, and the admin panel — and integrated Stripe for payments and Supabase for auth and media, working closely with a senior developer who led the backend architecture.

decisions.title: Booking flow with Stripe
decisions.body: I implemented the checkout and payment flow with Stripe, keeping the UI clear and reassuring at every step so users could complete a real booking with confidence.

decisions.title: Multi-language catalog (EN/ES)
decisions.body: I structured the catalog and content for two languages with SEO optimized for local searches, so the business could reach both local and international clients.

decisions.title: Supabase for auth and storage
decisions.body: I used Supabase for authentication and media storage, and Prisma over PostgreSQL for typed data access, collaborating with the senior developer on the data model.

decisions.title: Premium narrative and performance
decisions.body: I designed a cohesive premium experience with Framer Motion animations that stay smooth on mobile, and optimized metadata and asset loading for speed.

caseStudy.outcome: The platform is live in production for a real business, taking real bookings and payments — my strongest example of shipping a complete commercial product as the frontend lead alongside a senior backend developer.

items.title: CubanParty
items.subtitle: Events platform with interactive animations
items.description: An event promotion and management platform with a strong visual layer: interactive particle animations, smooth page transitions, and a content-management system backed by Appwrite. All media is served from AWS S3 with intelligent preloading. In production for a real business.
highlights: Particle animations with tsparticles synchronized to scroll
Smooth page transitions with Framer Motion (layout animations + AnimatePresence)
Serverless backend with Appwrite (auth, database, storage)
Media optimization with AWS S3 + viewport-based preloading

caseStudy.challenge: Create a memorable visual experience for an events platform without sacrificing performance on mobile devices, where most of the audience visits. The challenge was integrating complex particle animations with real-time data without frame drops.
caseStudy.solution: I combined tsparticles for background effects (with lightweight presets on mobile) and Framer Motion for UI transitions, clearly separating decorative animation from interactive motion. Data management with Appwrite + SWR keeps the app reactive without a custom backend.

decisions.title: Adaptive particle system
decisions.body: I implemented device capability detection to reduce particles and disable interactivity on mobile, keeping a consistent 60fps even on mid-range devices.

decisions.title: Appwrite as BaaS
decisions.body: I chose Appwrite over Firebase for its relational data model, cacheable REST API, and SDK that integrates cleanly with SWR for real-time data.

decisions.title: Layout animations with Framer Motion
decisions.body: I used layoutId for shared transitions between event lists and detail views, creating a cinematic feel without a performance penalty.

decisions.title: AWS S3 + on-the-fly transformations
decisions.body: I combined S3 with image transformations at the edge to serve modern formats (AVIF/WebP) based on the user's browser, reducing average image weight by 70%.

caseStudy.outcome: The platform achieved Lighthouse Performance 90+ on mobile despite the animations, and became a portfolio piece showing that performance and visual design can work together.

items.title: VOO Platform
items.subtitle: Multi-tenant SaaS frontend for e-commerce, bookings, and events
items.description: A multi-tenant SaaS platform that lets event, hospitality, and service businesses launch their own site in minutes. I built the frontend UI teamed with a senior developer: customer dashboard, global admin panel, public landing page, and a customizable template system serving multiple tenants from a single codebase.
highlights: Monorepo with 3 frontend apps and shared packages (UI, auth, queries, emails)
Custom domain support with dynamic tenant routing
Multi-language support with next-intl and dynamic theming configurable per business
Reusable, accessible component library (Radix UI + shadcn/ui)

caseStudy.challenge: Build the frontend of a multi-tenant SaaS from scratch, allowing three very different verticals (events, hospitality, and services) to offer a personalized end-user experience without duplicating code, while keeping accessibility and performance at an enterprise-product level.
caseStudy.solution: Teamed with a senior developer, I helped build a Turborepo monorepo architecture with three independent applications (customer dashboard, global admin, and landing page) sharing UI, authentication, query, and email-template packages. The template system lets each tenant customize colors, typography, language, and content blocks without touching code.

decisions.title: UI composition with Radix + shadcn/ui
decisions.body: I built an accessible component system (WAI-ARIA) reused across the 3 apps, with semantic CSS-variable tokens to support tenant theming at runtime without recompilation.

decisions.title: Internationalization with next-intl
decisions.body: I integrated next-intl with subdomain segmentation to serve different languages depending on each tenant's region, with automatic fallback and pluralization support.

decisions.title: Customizable template system
decisions.body: I built a template layer that lets each tenant configure theme, content blocks, and language, so a new business can launch without engineering work.

decisions.title: End-to-end typing
decisions.body: I shared API-generated types across the frontend apps, eliminating contract errors and giving full autocomplete for every call.

caseStudy.outcome: The product supports multiple tenants with a modular architecture that makes it possible to add new verticals in days, not weeks. Still in active development ahead of its public launch.

items.title: Dev Events
items.subtitle: Platform to discover tech events and networking
items.description: A personal project: an events platform for developers built with the latest Next.js features (App Router, Server Components) and MongoDB persistence, with a simple and fast experience for both users and content management.
highlights: Fast event discovery with filters and a clean interface
MongoDB-backed data layer and Vercel deployment
Mobile and desktop-first navigation experience
Live deployed project with public repository

items.title: Mojitos Bar
items.subtitle: Cocktail website with animated GSAP storytelling
items.description: A one-page cocktail bar website built as a personal showcase for advanced GSAP animations. Every section is choreographed with timelines and ScrollTriggers, demonstrating control over micro-interactions and visual storytelling.
highlights: GSAP timelines with ScrollTrigger in every section
Character-by-character text reveals synchronized with scroll
Multi-layer parallax and complex animation scrubbing
Responsive design that keeps its fluidity on mobile

items.title: Memecoin Dashboard
items.subtitle: Token trading interface with real-time visualization
items.description: Dashboard for managing and visualizing crypto token transactions. Complex forms with real-time validation, price evolution charts, and responsive design optimized for long sessions on large screens.
highlights: Multi-step forms with React Hook Form and instant validation
Real-time data visualization with optimistic updates
Responsive design optimized for long desktop sessions
Contextual notification system with Radix Toast
